sanskrit: यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च | अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धि: सा पार्थ राजसी ||
transliteration: yayā dharmam adharmaṃ ca kāryaṃ cākāryam eva ca | ayathāvat prajānāti buddhiḥ sā pārtha rājasī ||
literal_meaning: By which one mis-knows dharma and adharma, what ought to be done and what ought not to be done, that intellect, O Pārtha, is of the rājasic kind.
contextual_meaning: A mind clouded by rajas (passion/activation) distorts perception: values flip, priorities invert, and the person mistakes short-term excitement or social approval for genuine right action.
psychological_state: Hyper-aroused, reward-seeking cognition that overrides the slower, value-aligned prefrontal evaluation; resembles ADHD-like impulsivity or addictive craving.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m., buying a limited-drop sneaker you can’t afford, then justifying it as ‘self-care’ while ignoring tomorrow’s rent.
anchor_text: Pause before acting; rajas makes right and wrong look alike.
